danger_signals.visual_form: Grid-based unit icons in contrasting faction colors on tactical map
danger_signals.meaning: Threat to player forces; indicates need for defensive positioning or offensive action

danger_signals.visual_form: Reduced hit points display on unit stat panel
danger_signals.meaning: Unit vulnerability; risk of elimination if attacked

danger_signals.visual_form: Primary weapon ammo counter reaching zero on unit interface
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of primary offensive capability; unit forced to secondary weapon with reduced effectiveness

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy units positioned adjacent to or on player's headquarters tile
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent strategic defeat; HQ capture results in mission loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Unit standing on unfavorable terrain (plains vs. woods) with visible defense modifier
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced defensive capability; unit takes increased damage in this position

safety_signals.visual_form: Multiple units of same faction color clustered together on map
safety_signals.meaning: Defensive strength through numbers; concentrated forces provide mutual protection

safety_signals.visual_form: Unit positioned on woods or mountain terrain with visible defense bonus
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced defensive position; unit takes reduced damage from attacks

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly faction flag or color overlay on property tile
safety_signals.meaning: Resource generation and strategic position held; contributes to unit supply and funding

safety_signals.visual_form: Full or near-full hit points on unit stat display
safety_signals.meaning: Combat readiness; unit can sustain multiple engagements

safety_signals.visual_form: Primary weapon ammo counter at full capacity
safety_signals.meaning: Full offensive capability; unit can engage at maximum effectiveness

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Orange unit sprites and UI elements distinguish from other factions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orange flag/overlay on captured cities, bases, airports, ports

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Blue unit sprites and UI elements visually distinct
Yellow unit sprites and interface elements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yellow flag/overlay on captured terrain features

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Green unit sprites and UI elements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Green flag/overlay on controlled properties and HQ

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active regional conflict between two nations
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map displays multiple unit types from both factions positioned across terrain; faction colors indicate territorial control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units engage in turn-based combat; armies build new units at bases; strategic positioning reflects military campaign progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Four faction colors visible simultaneously on campaign maps; units from Orange Star, Blue Moon, Yellow Comet, and Green Earth co-exist
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alliances and enemies shift; commanding officers from different nations appear; diplomatic narrative reflected in mission briefings

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Units concentrated in woods, mountains, and fortified positions (bases, HQ); minimal unit movement across open terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players adopt defensive strategies; terrain defense bonuses become critical; attrition-based gameplay dominates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited city/port control reduces resource generation; unit ammunition reserves display as depleted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fewer new units produced per turn; existing units must resort to secondary weapons; strategic focus on capturing income-generating properties

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player faction units spread across map; multiple properties captured and controlled; HQ position secure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapid unit production; aggressive movement into enemy territory; frequent successful combat engagements

environmental_storytelling.example: Captured cities, ports, airports, and bases display faction colors. The distribution and density of faction-controlled properties on the map tells the story of which nation controls which regions, indicating military progress and strategic dominance

environmental_storytelling.technique: Unit type diversity reflects military doctrine
environmental_storytelling.example: Different unit types (Infantry, Mech, Tank, Helicopter, Lander, Shoal Cruiser) positioned across terrain types communicate faction military capabilities and strategic focus—infantry in urban areas, vehicles on plains, naval units near ports and shoals

Woods, mountains, and shoals form defensive chokepoints and strongholds. Their occupation tells the story of fortified positions and contested boundaries between factions. Empty plains indicate uncontested or rapidly-changing territory

The headquarters location is the ultimate prize. Its centrality, remoteness, or isolation on the map communicates strategic vulnerability or safety. Proximity of enemy forces to HQ escalates narrative tension toward decisive victory/defeat

Proliferation of captured resource-generating properties indicates which faction has economic dominance. Contested or neutral properties represent economic battlegrounds; their visual appearance signals income generation potential and strategic value

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can perform direct or indirect attacks; moves across varied terrain; captures properties; maintains offensive capability
Resorts to secondary weapon (machine gun); reduced threat level; typically held in defensive position; prioritized for repair if near base
Primary threat to enemy vehicles; direct attack engagement; prioritized targeting of tanks and other mechs
Significantly enhanced defense stat; acts as mobile fortress; can hold territory against multiple lighter units
No terrain advantage or disadvantage; maintains uniform defense; mobility-focused behavior; used for rapid repositioning or indirect fire support
Engaged in territorial water control; indirect fire attack pattern; supports amphibious operations via Lander units
Ferries infantry across water; enables beach landings; vulnerable to naval/air units while in transport mode
Produces new units each turn; resupplies ammo and repairs units stationed on tile; generates income funds
No unit production for player; enemy generates income and supplies; strategic priority for capture or destruction
Cannot be captured; loss results in mission defeat; primary strategic focus of defense; captures provide instant victory condition

notes: Super Famicom Wars (1998) predates Advance Wars (2001) in the Wars series. The available sources primarily document Advance Wars, which shares the same tactical grid-based turn-strategy paradigm and visual communication system. Visual faction differentiation through color-coding (Orange Star, Blue Moon, Yellow Comet, Green Earth) is core to player comprehension of battlefield state. The turn-based grid system transforms terrain into visual narrative—woods/mountains become defensive strongholds, plains become vulnerable transition zones. Unit sprite variation by type and faction creates a visual vocabulary where players instantly read threat level, capability, and allegiance. Property control (cities, bases, ports, airports) visualized through faction-color overlay communicates economic and strategic power distribution across the map. The HQ tile functions as the ultimate narrative marker—its safety or threat directly communicates campaign momentum and narrative tension.
